app.title: ARCHIPELAGO
app.subtitle: A Social Contract Management Game
app.footer: This game is inspired by the CO3 — Continuous Construction of Resilient Social Contracts project (grant no. 101132631), supported by the European Union's Horizon Europe programme.

onboard.intro: Every society rests on a fragile six-dimensional balance. This balance is never fixed — it shifts under crisis and is shaped by decisions. Tap each card to understand a dimension. Then choose an island.
onboard.goal: The goal isn't to maximize any single dimension — it's to build a balanced and resilient whole.

select.intro: Each island has its own strengths and vulnerabilities — the same policy won't produce the same outcome everywhere.

lesson.coRaise: ✦ not zero-sum: two dimensions rose together
lesson.tradeoff: trade-off: strengthening one dimension touched another

final.criticalNote: Cohesion and stability were built at the expense of excluded groups' rights and sense of justice. A contract that looks solid on the surface can rot from within.
final.learningTitle: What You Experienced in This Game

learning.a: A social contract isn't one single thing. You tracked six dimensions separately — managing one didn't mean managing another.
learning.b: Context is decisive. In {{count}} decisions, the same policy produced different outcomes depending on the island's conditions.
learning.c: It isn't zero-sum. {{count}} times you raised two dimensions at once — with no one paying the cost.
learning.c2: It isn't zero-sum — but you didn't catch that moment in this run. Try another island.
learning.d: Trade-offs are real. In {{count}} decisions, strengthening one dimension touched another.
learning.e: Your decisions echo. {{count}} times, an issue you postponed came back to meet you.
learning.f: The cycle never closes. Your last decision was "{{choice}}." Every decision lays the ground for the next.
